quranic_basis.relevance: Direct: God raised the heaven and set the balance, so do not transgress in the balance; establish weight in justice and do not skimp the balance.
Direct: messengers and the Book and the Balance were sent so people may uphold justice.
Direct: God commands justice (ʿadl) and excellence (iḥsān).
Direct: stand firmly for justice as witnesses for God, even against yourselves or kin.
Direct: let not hatred of a people lead you away from justice.
Direct: render trusts (amānāt) to whom they are due, and judge with justice.
Direct: the trust was offered to the heavens, earth, and mountains; humanity bore it.
Direct: the believers are those faithful to their trusts and covenants.
Direct: the Messenger sent as a mercy to all the worlds.
Direct: God has prescribed mercy upon Himself.
Direct: God grants wisdom to whom He wills, and whoever is granted wisdom is granted much good.
Direct: invite to the path with wisdom and good counsel.
Direct: God commands justice and iḥsān (excellence).
Direct: do good; God loves the doers of good (muḥsinīn).
Direct: the most noble before God is the most mindful/righteous (atqā).
Direct: take provision, but the best provision is taqwā.
Direct: truth (ḥaqq) has come and falsehood has vanished; falsehood is ever bound to vanish.
Direct: do not mix truth with falsehood nor conceal the truth knowingly.
Direct: do not consume one another's wealth wrongfully (bil-bāṭil).
Direct: do not clothe truth with falsehood (bāṭil).
Direct: corruption (fasād) has appeared on land and sea by what people's hands have earned.
Direct: do not cause corruption on the earth after its reform.
Direct: he strives to spread corruption, destroying crops and progeny.
Direct: 'I only intend reform (iṣlāḥ) as much as I am able.'
Direct: do not corrupt the earth after its reform (iṣlāḥ).
Direct: blame lies on those who wrong people and transgress on the earth without right.
Direct: God does not like the public mention of evil except by one who has been wronged.
Direct: eat and drink but do not be excessive (lā tusrifū); God does not love the excessive.
Direct: do not squander wastefully; the squanderers are brethren of the devils.
Direct: do not waste; God does not love the wasteful.
Direct: their affairs are decided by consultation (shūrā) among them.
Direct: consult them in the matter.
Direct: We place the scales of justice for the Day of Reckoning; no soul is wronged in the least.
Direct: God will call you to account for what is in yourselves.
Direct: record debts in writing and call witnesses to transactions.
Direct: be witnesses for God, even against yourselves.
Direct: documentation and witnessing as clear proof of obligations.
Direct: 'I am upon clear evidence (bayyinah) from my Lord.'
Direct: there is no creature on earth but its provision (rizq) is upon God.
Direct: walk the earth and eat of His provision.
Direct: do not consume your wealth (amwāl) among yourselves wrongfully.
Direct: do not give the foolish your property which God made a means of support.
Direct: God permitted trade and forbade ribā; relinquish remaining ribā.
Direct: do not consume ribā doubled and multiplied.

Indirect anchor: prohibition of consuming wealth wrongfully underpins the principle; the specific gharar doctrine is primarily developed from prophetic tradition and jurisprudence, not a direct Quranic term.
Indirect anchor: trade must be by mutual consent, which excessive uncertainty undermines.
Direct: intoxicants and gambling (maysir) are abominations; avoid them.
Direct: in wine and gambling there is great sin and some benefit, but the sin is greater.
Direct: O you who believe, fulfil (awfū) your contracts.
Direct: fulfil the covenant; the covenant will be questioned.
Direct: remember the covenant (mīthāq) by which He bound you.
Direct: they have taken from you a solemn covenant (mīthāqan ghalīẓā).
Direct: establish weight in equity (qisṭ) and do not skimp the balance.
Direct: the Book and Balance sent so people uphold equity (qisṭ).
Direct: We made you a balanced/middle community (ummatan wasaṭā).
Direct: the servants of the Merciful spend neither extravagantly nor stingily, but between.
Direct: 'I will place upon the earth a successive steward (khalīfah).'
Direct: He made you stewards of the earth and tests you in what He has given.
Direct: do not cause corruption on the earth (al-arḍ) after its reform.
Direct: He made the earth a resting place and sent down water to bring forth fruits as provision.
Direct: We made from water every living thing.
Direct: He sends pure water to give life to dead land and to give drink.
Direct: whoever saves one life, it is as if he saved all mankind.
Direct: do not take a life which God has made sacred, except by right.
Direct: he spreads corruption, destroying crops and progeny (nasl); God does not love corruption.
Direct: God grants offspring as He wills, a trust and continuity of generations.
Direct: 'Had we listened or reasoned (naʿqil), we would not be among the people of the Fire.'
Direct: in creation are signs for people who reason (yaʿqilūn).
Direct: there is no compulsion in religion (dīn).
Direct: 'For you your religion, and for me mine.'
Direct: seek help through patience (ṣabr) and prayer.
Direct: those who enjoin truth and enjoin patience are saved from loss.
Direct: 'If you are grateful, I will surely increase you.'
Direct: if you tried to count God's favours you could not enumerate them.
Direct: they were commanded to worship God with sincere devotion (mukhliṣīn).
Direct: worship God, sincere to Him in religion; sincere devotion is due to God alone.
Indirect anchor: God holds you to account for what your hearts have earned (intent of the heart); the maxim 'actions are by intentions' is primarily from prophetic tradition.
Indirect anchor: there is no blame for mistakes, but for what the hearts intend.
Direct: be mindful of God and be with the truthful (ṣādiqīn).
Direct: the truthful men and women have prepared for them forgiveness and great reward.
Direct: do not betray (lā takhūnū) God and the Messenger, nor betray your trusts knowingly.
Direct: if you fear betrayal from a people, deal with them equitably; God does not love the treacherous.
Direct: woe to those who give short measure (muṭaffifīn), who take full measure but skimp when giving.
Direct: give full measure and do not be of those who cause loss; do not deprive people of their due.
Direct: give orphans their property and do not exchange the bad for the good or consume their wealth.
Direct: do not oppress the orphan.
Direct: righteousness includes giving wealth to the needy (masākīn).
Direct: woe to those who repel the orphan and do not urge feeding the needy.
Direct: righteousness includes giving wealth to the wayfarer (ibn as-sabīl).
Direct: charity is for the poor, the needy, and the wayfarer, among others.
Direct: be good to parents; speak to them honourably and lower to them the wing of humility.
Direct: We enjoined upon man care for his parents.
Direct: those who strive (jāhadū) for Us, We will guide to Our ways.
Direct: strive against them with it (the Quran) a great striving — striving by argument and conviction.
Direct: make reconciliation (aṣliḥū) between believers in conflict, with justice.
Direct: reconciliation (ṣulḥ) is best.
Direct: let them worship the Lord who fed them against hunger and secured them against fear.
Direct: grant protection (amān) to one who seeks it, then convey him to safety.
Direct: 'My Lord, increase me in knowledge (ʿilm).'
Direct: do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge; hearing, sight, and heart will be questioned.
Direct: they reflect (yatafakkarūn) on the creation of the heavens and the earth.
Direct: such examples We present that they may reflect.
Direct: do they not contemplate deeply (yatadabbarūn) the Quran, or are there locks upon hearts?
Direct: do they not contemplate (yatadabbarūn)? Had it been from other than God they would find much discrepancy.
Indirect anchor: reform/benefit (iṣlāḥ) of the orphans' affairs is praised; maṣlaḥah as a juristic doctrine is derived from such benefit-oriented texts, not a direct Quranic term.
Indirect anchor: prohibition of corruption after reform implies the protection of benefit.
Indirect anchor: spreading corruption (fasād) destroying crops and progeny; mafsadah is the juristic category derived from fasād texts.
Indirect anchor: corruption appearing on land and sea by human action.
Direct: do not retain them to cause harm (ḍirāran) and transgress.
Direct: let neither scribe nor witness be harmed (lā yuḍārra).
Indirect anchor: the Quran prohibits harming to transgress; the maxim 'lā ḍarar wa lā ḍirār' is primarily a prophetic tradition forming a legal maxim, not a direct Quranic verse.
Direct: saving one life is as saving all mankind; foundational to the preservation-of-life objective.
Direct: do not kill the soul God has made sacred except by right.
Direct: do not consume wealth among yourselves wrongfully; anchors protection of property.
Direct: do not consume one another's wealth unjustly, but by mutual trade.
Direct: intoxicants are an abomination to avoid; classically anchors protection of the intellect.
Direct: God gave you hearing, sight, and hearts (intellect) that you may be grateful.
Direct: no compulsion in religion; anchors freedom of conscience as a protected objective.
Direct: were it not for people repelling others, places of worship would be destroyed.
Direct: corruption destroying crops and progeny (nasl) is condemned; anchors protection of progeny.
Direct: let those fear who would leave behind weak offspring and worry for them.
Indirect anchor: the earth spread out with everything in measured balance (mawzūn); tawāzun is derived from the Quranic balance vocabulary.
Indirect anchor: no flaw or disproportion in creation, implying maintained equilibrium.
Direct: these are the limits of God (ḥudūd Allāh), so do not transgress them.
Direct: whoever transgresses the limits of God has wronged himself.
Direct: indeed, We created everything in due measure (bi-qadar).
Direct: everything with Him is by due measure (bi-miqdār).
Direct: so that wealth does not circulate only among the rich among you; anchors distributive social justice.
Direct: God commands justice and excellence and giving to kin (a social ordering).
Direct: in their wealth is a known right (ḥaqq maʿlūm) for the petitioner and the deprived.
Direct: give the relative his right (ḥaqq), and the needy and the wayfarer.
Indirect anchor: fulfil your contracts; the category of binding duties (wājibāt) is a juristic systematisation of such commands.
Indirect anchor: fulfil the covenant — covenants will be questioned.
Indirect anchor: one delegated to act on the group's behalf (sending one with their money); wakālah is the juristic contract derived from such delegation.
Indirect anchor: appointment of a trustworthy agent over resources (Yūsuf over the storehouses).
Indirect anchor: bonds of protective alliance/guardianship (walāyah) among believers.
Indirect anchor: render trusts and judge justly when you hold authority; wilāyah is constrained by amānah and ʿadl.
Direct: hearing, sight, and heart — each of these will be questioned (masʾūlā).
Direct: stop them, for they are to be questioned (masʾūlūn).
Indirect anchor: the just scales of reckoning (ḥisāb); muḥāsabah is the active practice derived from the ḥisāb principle.
Indirect anchor: let every soul look to what it has sent ahead — a call to self-accounting.
Direct: God is ever a Watcher (Raqīb) over you; oversight (raqābah) derives from this attribute applied to human institutions.
Direct: no word is uttered but there is a vigilant observer (raqīb ʿatīd).
Direct: God will surely grant them succession (yastakhlifannahum) on the earth — authority as a conditional trust.
Direct: spend from that of which He made you trustees/successors (mustakhlafīn).
Direct: He brought you forth from the earth and settled you to develop it (istaʿmarakum fīhā).
Direct: He subjected (sakhkhara) to you all that is in the heavens and earth, as a favour.
Direct: He subjected the rivers, sun, moon, and ships for your benefit.
Direct: if you counted God's favours (niʿmah) you could not enumerate them.
Direct: if you are grateful for the favour I will increase you; if ungrateful, My punishment is severe.
Direct: had the people believed and been mindful, We would have opened blessings (barakāt) from heaven and earth.
Direct: He placed therein blessings (bāraka) and measured its sustenance.
Direct: eat of what is lawful and wholesome (ḥalālan ṭayyiban) on the earth.
Direct: He makes lawful for them the wholesome things (ṭayyibāt) and forbids the impure.
Direct: and He forbids them the impure/harmful things (khabāʾith).
Direct: the impure (khabīth) and the wholesome are not equal, even if the abundance of the impure impresses you.
Direct: eat of what is lawful (ḥalāl) and wholesome.
Direct: eat of what God has provided you, lawful and good.
Direct: say, come, I will recite what your Lord has forbidden (ḥarrama) upon you.
Direct: my Lord has only forbidden immoralities, sin, oppression, and associating partners.
Indirect anchor: avoid much suspicion; the lawful-doubtful-prohibited tripartite is primarily a prophetic tradition, not a direct Quranic verse.
Direct: establish prayer and give the obligatory due (zakāh).
Direct: zakāh is for the poor, the needy, its administrators, and other defined categories.
Direct: do not nullify your charities (ṣadaqāt) with reminders of generosity or injury.
Direct: if you disclose charities it is good, but to give them to the poor in secret is better.
Indirect anchor: the parable of a grain growing seven ears — enduring multiplied benefit; waqf is a juristic institution built on such perpetual-charity principles, not a direct Quranic term.
Indirect anchor: you will not attain righteousness until you spend from what you love.
Direct: who will lend God a goodly loan (qarḍan ḥasanan) that He may multiply it.
Direct: if you lend God a goodly loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you.
Direct: when you contract a debt (dayn) for a fixed term, write it down.
Direct: if the debtor is in hardship, grant respite until ease; to remit it as charity is better.
Direct: God has permitted trade (al-bayʿ) and forbidden usury.
Direct: do not consume wealth unjustly, but let it be trade by mutual consent.
Direct: hire him; the best to hire is the strong and trustworthy — and a marriage in exchange for hired service (ijārah).
Direct: if they nurse for you, give them their due payment.
Indirect anchor: many partners (khulaṭāʾ) wrong one another except the just; mushārakah is the juristic contract built on partnership principles.
Indirect anchor: heirs are partners (shurakāʾ) in the estate — shared entitlement in proportion.
Indirect anchor: others travel the earth seeking God's bounty (yaḍribūn fī-l-arḍ); muḍārabah is the juristic contract derived from such enterprise, not a direct Quranic term.
Indirect anchor: disperse and seek God's bounty after the prayer.
Direct: 'My father invites you to reward you with a wage (ajr) for having watered for us.'
Direct: give them their wage (ujūr) for nursing.
Indirect anchor: those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend it in God's cause face severe punishment; the specific prohibition of iḥtikār of essentials derives mainly from prophetic tradition.
Indirect anchor: woe to those who deal in fraud of measure; najash (sham bidding) is prohibited primarily by prophetic tradition as a form of deceptive dealing.
Direct: woe to those who give short measure (muṭaffifīn), who take full from others but skimp when they measure or weigh for them.
Direct: give full measure and weight in justice and do not deprive people of their due.
Direct: do not squander wastefully (tabdhīr); the squanderers are brethren of the devils.
Direct: let not those who are miserly (yabkhalūn) with what God gave them think it is good for them.
Direct: those who are miserly and enjoin miserliness on people and conceal what God has given them.
Direct: I will turn away from My signs those who are arrogant (yatakabbarūn) in the land without right.
Direct: thus does God seal the heart of every arrogant tyrant.
Direct: and from the evil of an envier (ḥāsid) when he envies.
Direct: or do they envy people for what God has given them of His bounty?
Direct: do not backbite one another (lā yaghtab); would one like to eat the flesh of his dead brother?
Direct: woe to every backbiter and slanderer (humazah lumazah).
Direct: condemnation of the slanderer who goes about with malicious talebearing (mashshāʾin bi-namīm).
Direct: if a corrupt person brings you news, verify it, lest you harm people in ignorance.
Direct: persecution/strife (fitnah) is worse than killing — marking fitnah as a grave social harm.
Direct: beware of a trial (fitnah) that will not strike only the wrongdoers among you.

Inferred: condemnation of corrupting 'crops and progeny' implies protection of productive soil.

Inferred: 'He sent down water and caused gardens of beauty to grow' grounds the worth of restoring green cover.

Inferred: 'a known right for the deprived' supports access for those who cannot afford it.
Inferred: the Yūsuf storage model supports reserves and resilience against supply shocks.
Inferred: being 'secured against fear' extends to security of essential supply.

Inferred: condemnation of squandering (tabdhīr) applies to wasteful energy use.
Inferred: spending from 'that of which He made you trustees (mustakhlafīn)' grounds resource trusteeship.
Inferred: subjected resources (taskhīr) held responsibly, not exploited without limit.
Inferred: the ethic against waste (isrāf) thematically supports closing material loops; circular economy itself is a modern construct.
Inferred: condemnation of squandering resonates with eliminating designed waste.
Inferred: 'do not waste; He does not love the wasteful' directly grounds waste minimisation.
Inferred: avoidance of squandering applied to material throughput.

Inferred: 'hearing, sight, and heart will be questioned' grounds accountability for foreseeable effects.
Inferred: planning ahead for lean years models proactive adaptation to coming stress.
Inferred: concern for leaving descendants secure grounds protecting people from climate harm.
Inferred: 'fed them against hunger' marks freedom from hunger as a basic good to secure.
Inferred: Yūsuf's grain-storage strategy models securing food against scarcity.
Inferred: 'eat of its fruit, give its due, and do not waste' grounds productive yet non-wasteful farming.
Inferred: the contrast of good and barren land grounds care for productive capacity.
Inferred: bringing 'dead land to life' resonates with regenerating soil and ecosystems.
Inferred: reform (iṣlāḥ) over corruption supports net-restorative farming.
Direct (underlying principle), modern framing: animals as 'communities like you' grounds duties of care toward them.
Inferred: cattle created 'for warmth and benefit' alongside beauty implies respectful, non-cruel use.
Inferred: 'God is the splitter of grain and seed' grounds the value of the seed as a basis of provision.
Inferred: 'We caused grain to grow' as provision implies safeguarding its source.

Direct: 'let there be a community enjoining the good (al-maʿrūf) and forbidding the wrong'.
Direct: believing men and women 'enjoin al-maʿrūf and forbid al-munkar'.

Direct: 'whoever relies on God, He is sufficient for him'; the action-pairing ('tie your camel and trust') is from prophetic tradition.
Direct: 'the work of God who perfected/mastered (atqana) everything' grounds the value of mastery in work.
